case_title: KATHERINE FRAZER VS. DR. VIC PRIETO et al

docket_entries.description: COURT REPORTING SERVICES LESS THAN 1 HOUR FILED BY PLAINTIFF FRAZER, KATHERINE

MASTER MOTION CALENDAR ON DEC-04-2013 IN 206, HEARING ON APPLICANT/PLAINTIFF KATHERINE J. FRAZER'S WAIVER OF COURT FEES AND COSTS. NO APPEARANCE BY APPLICANT. THE COURT BEING UNABLE TO EXAMINE APPLICANT RULED ON THE APPLICATION SUBMITTED. THE COURT GRANTED THE APPLICATION IN WHOLE. ORDER SIGNED, AND ENDORSED-FILED COPY MAILED TO APPLICANT. JUDGE: A. LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT STEPHEN VAN PELT, M.D.'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. RULING: THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: SUSTAINED WITH 30 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. (CCP SEC. 430.10(E) AND (F).) AS TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION, PLAINTIFF IS GIVEN LEAVE TO AMEND TO ALLEGE A MEDICAL NEGLIGENCE CAUSE OF ACTION AGAINST DEFENDANTS. PLAINTIFF IS ALSO GIVEN LEAVE TO AMEND TO CLARIFY WHICH DOCTOR TREATED HER, FOR WHAT REASON SHE SOUGHT TREATMENT FROM EACH DOCTOR, AND WHAT TREATMENT WAS ACTUALLY RECEIVED. AS TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION, PLAINTIFF IS GIVEN LEAVE TO AMEND TO ALLEGE, IF SHE CAN IN GOOD FAITH, FACTS ESTABLISHING A FRAUD CAUSE OF ACTION. AS TO THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION, PLAINTIFF IS GIVEN LEAVE TO AMEND TO ALLEGE, IF SHE CAN IN GOOD FAITH, FACTS ESTABLISHING A SLANDER CAUSE OF ACTION. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER CSR5976

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT STEPHEN VAN PELT, M.D.'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. RULING: THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DENIED AS MOOT, IN LIGHT OF THE RULING ON DEMURRER. DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-03-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Marcell Patrick Neri, Esq., 415.288.9800, for Defendants Hoyman Hong, MD and Stephen Van Pelt, MD; No other appearances.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT STEPHEN VAN PELT, M.D.'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained with 30 days leave to amend. (CCP sec. 430.10(e) and (f).) As to the first cause of action, Plaintiff is given leave to amend to allege a medical negligence cause of action against defendants. Plaintiff is also given leave to amend to clarify which doctor treated her, for what reason she sought treatment from each doctor, and what treatment was actually received. As to the second cause of action, Plaintiff is given leave to amend to allege, if she can in good faith, facts establishing a fraud cause of action. As to the third cause of action, Plaintiff is given leave to amend to allege, if she can in good faith, facts establishing a slander cause of action. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR5976
